label: The Prismwright Exchange
description: The Prismwright Exchange is a narrow-fronted shop cut into Seamline Concord’s basalt-glass terrace wall, its façade laminated with mirror-slag that holds the copper-white twilight in disciplined bands. Interior shelving rises in stepped racks, each tier grounded by antistatic fins that keep Lacrymara’s charged glass dust from fogging the wares and lenses. A long calibration counter of dark vitrified stone runs parallel to the avenue sightlines, its inlaid seam-line marks matching the city’s navigational geometry. Caged lightwells admit attenuated radiance from above, turning the room into an exacting chamber for optical trade and measurement.
